# Copilot Instructions — FH6 Car Data

## Overview

A mobile-first PWA (Progressive Web App) to look up **Forza Horizon 6** car stats and auction prices. React 18 + Vite 5 frontend; Flask 3 API backed by static JSON files (`data/cars.json`, `data/parts.json`). No database — all data lives in JSON files loaded into memory at startup.

## Build, Test & Lint Commands

CI is disabled; run all checks locally.

### Python (API)

```bash
# Run all tests
python3 -m pytest tests/ -q

# Run a single test
python3 -m pytest tests/test_api.py::test_health -v

# Lint and format
ruff check .
ruff format --check .
```

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm run lint          # ESLint (--max-warnings 0, so any warning fails)
npm run build         # Vite production build (also generates public/version.json)
npm run dev           # Dev server on :3002, proxies /api → :5002
```

### Combined test runner

```bash
./run-tests.sh            # Python lint + pytest + frontend lint + build
./run-tests.sh python     # Python only
./run-tests.sh frontend   # Frontend only
```

> **Ports:** API defaults to `FH6_API_PORT=5002`, UI to `FH6_UI_PORT=3002`.

## Architecture

```
fh6cardata/
├── api/main.py          # Single-file Flask app — all routes here
├── data/
│   ├── cars.json        # Car catalogue (loaded once, cached in _cars global)
│   └── parts.json       # Tuning parts (loaded once, cached in _parts global)
├── frontend/src/
│   ├── App.jsx          # Root: fetch/filter/sort state, owns all API calls
│   ├── components/      # Presentational — CarCard, CarGrid, SearchFilters, etc.
│   └── hooks/           # useOwnedCars (localStorage), useDebounce, useAppVersion
├── scripts/scrape_wiki.py  # One-off data refresh from Forza Fandom wiki
└── tests/test_api.py    # pytest tests using Flask test client
```

**Data flow:** `App.jsx` fetches `/api/cars` with query params on every filter change (debounced 300 ms for the text query). Owned-car tracking and client-side sorting are applied after the API response — they are never sent to the API. Filter options come from `/api/filters`.

**Auction pricing** is computed server-side in `api/main.py` using `AUCTION_TIERS` multipliers on the car's `base_value`, rounded to the nearest 1,000 CR.

## Key Conventions

### API (Python)

- All routes live in `api/main.py` — there is no routes package in use.
- List endpoints return via `_etag_response()` which adds an `ETag` + `Cache-Control: no-cache` header; use it for any new list/bulk endpoint.
- Query params use Flask's `request.args`; filter params are stripped and lowercased/uppercased before comparison.
- `pi_class` values are uppercase: `D`, `C`, `B`, `A`, `S1`, `S2`, `R`.
- Rarity values are title-case: `Common`, `Rare`, `Epic`, `Legendary`.
- Auction endpoints return HTTP 200 even for non-auctionable cars (with `"auctionable": false`) — not 4xx.
- `ruff` line length is 100; quote style is double. Run `ruff format .` before committing.

### Frontend

- `API_BASE = '/api'` is defined at the top of `App.jsx` — use it everywhere, never hardcode a URL.
- Filtering is done server-side (query params to `/api/cars`). Owned-car filter and sorting are client-side only (`useMemo` in `App.jsx`).
- `useOwnedCars` persists to `localStorage` under key `fh6_owned_cars` (array of car IDs).
- `useAppVersion` polls `/version.json` every 5 min; `version.json` is generated by the `buildVersionPlugin` in `vite.config.js` at every build.
- Workbox cache names follow the pattern `fh6-<resource>-api` (e.g. `fh6-cars-api`). The "Refresh Data" button in `App.jsx` deletes all caches matching `fh6-*-api`.
- ESLint is configured with `--max-warnings 0`; zero warnings are allowed.

### Car data schema (required fields)

```
id, full_name, manufacturer, model, year, rarity, pi_class, pi,
base_value, auctionable, availability, country, stats{}, image_url
```

### Parts data schema (required fields)

```
id, category, subcategory, name, tier, applies_to_classes[], stat_effects{}, pi_impact, notes
```

## PWA / iOS Update Strategy

The app uses four complementary mechanisms to push updates to iOS home-screen installs:
1. `skipWaiting + clientsClaim` in the service worker — new SW activates immediately.
2. In-app `UpdateBanner` shown when a new SW is waiting.
3. `/version.json` polled every 5 min (`NetworkOnly`, never cached).
4. `NetworkFirst` for HTML navigation with a 3 s network timeout.

When changing the update flow, be careful not to break any of these four mechanisms.
